summary: O'Neil promotes her Occupy Wall Street offshoot group, the Alternative Banking group, which continues to meet weekly at Columbia University. The group maintains several ongoing projects including essay series at Huffington Post and Occupy Summer School. She highlights their curated reading list as a valuable resource for quality financial and economic articles. The post serves primarily as an invitation to engage with the group's work and resources.
key_points: The Alternative Banking group emerged from Occupy Wall Street and still meets weekly at Columbia
The group produces essays for Huffington Post and runs Occupy Summer School
They maintain a curated reading list with archives going back two years
O'Neil considers the reading list higher quality than most articles she encounters
money_quotes: I know I waste my time every week reading lots of articles that are not nearly the quality of the ones listed on this reading list.
I think it's already pretty amazing what the group has accomplished, and I'm proud to be part of it.
key_insight: Sustained grassroots financial reform efforts like the Alternative Banking group continue their work long after the initial protest movement fades from headlines.
